ingredients.name: Kissako Katsute
ingredients.entityContext: This traditional Takayama cafe's wooden interior and atmospheric setting made it the model for the coffee shop Oreki and Chitanda visit in episode 10, where Oreki reluctantly accompanies Chitanda on one of her curious errands.

ingredients.name: Kamoso Kachoen Garden Park
ingredients.entityContext: The lush greenhouse of this Kakegawa flower park served as the visual basis for the botanical garden scenes in episode 4, where Eru Chitanda's wide-eyed wonder at the exotic plants perfectly mirrors her inquisitive personality.

ingredients.name: Ipponsugi Hakusan Shrine
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒506-0012 Gifu, Takayama, Hachikenmachi, 3 Chome−9, 八軒町2丁目6
ingredients.entityContext: This small shrine in Takayama's old town district appears in episode 2 as the backdrop for an early scene with Oreki and Chitanda exploring the neighborhood, capturing the quiet, historical atmosphere that defines their school town.

ingredients.name: Hirayu-no-Yu
ingredients.address: 24 Okuhida Onsengo Hirayu, Takayama, Gifu 506-1433, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The open-air hot spring at Hirayu Onsen appears in episode 7 during the class trip arc, giving Oreki and his friends a rare moment of relaxation and honest conversation away from Kamiyama High School mysteries.

ingredients.name: Hirayu Shrine
ingredients.address: 24-1 Okuhida Onsengo Hirayu, Takayama, Gifu 506-1433, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Located steps from the Hirayu hot springs, this small shrine is featured in episode 7 when the Classic Literature Club members wander the Okuhida resort area, adding a quiet spiritual note to the school trip episode.

ingredients.name: Hie Shrine
ingredients.localName: 飛騨山王宮 日枝神社
ingredients.entityContext: Hie Shrine, one of Takayama's most prominent Shinto sanctuaries, is depicted in the opening sequence as part of the town's living scenery that frames Oreki's daily wanderings through Kamiyama.

ingredients.name: Hidasoja
ingredients.entityContext: The Hida Soja shrine complex appears in episode 21 as a gathering point during the Kanya Festival arc, where its grounds and stone lanterns echo the festival atmosphere that becomes central to Chitanda's emotional plea to Oreki.

ingredients.name: Akiba Shrine Honmachi 4-chome
ingredients.localName: 秋葉神社 本町4丁目
ingredients.entityContext: This small Honmachi roadside shrine appears in episode 11 as Oreki passes through the shopping district, its compact presence among the old merchant townscape reflecting the layered historical texture of Kamiyama.

ingredients.name: Akiha Shrine
ingredients.entityContext: The Akiha Shrine near Shimosannomachi is shown in the opening credits as part of the atmospheric patchwork of Takayama shrines that define the Classic Literature Club's hometown.

ingredients.name: Alps Kaido Hirayu
ingredients.address: 628 Okuhida Onsengo Hirayu, Takayama, Gifu 506-1433, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This rest-stop complex at the Hirayu bus terminal appears in episode 7 when the club arrives at the Okuhida Onsen resort, serving as the entry point to the episode's mountain hot-spring setting.

ingredients.name: Ararate
ingredients.address: カフェレストランア・ラ・ラ, 14-53 Uzumasa Tayabucho, Ukyo Ward, Kyoto, 616-8167, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Honmachi-area eatery near the covered shopping arcade is referenced in episode 10 as part of the downtown Takayama streets Chitanda leads Oreki through during her food-related investigation.

ingredients.name: Ashinaga Statue
ingredients.entityContext: The distinctive Ashinaga-Tenaga (long-legs, long-arms) statues along the Miyagawa riverbank appear in episode 18, where Oreki notices them during a walk and they underline the quirky local folklore embedded in Kamiyama's streetscape.

ingredients.name: Bagpipe
ingredients.entityContext: This old-fashioned Takayama coffee shop served as the model for the retro cafe Oreki and Satoshi visit in episode 2, where Satoshi enthuses about the Classics Club's founding mystery over a warm drink.

ingredients.name: Bar Thumbs Up!
ingredients.entityContext: Appearing in episode 5, this Honmachi bar's distinctive facade provides the backdrop for an adult establishment Oreki walks past while tracing clues through Kamiyama's nighttime streets.

ingredients.name: COREO
ingredients.entityContext: The Honmachi shopfront of this local goods store appears in episode 10 as part of the downtown Takayama shopping street Chitanda and Oreki traverse, its traditional storefront blending into the old-town atmosphere.

ingredients.name: Chidori Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: This Miyagawa riverbank bridge appears in episode 18 and is one of several Takayama bridges the show uses to frame quiet outdoor conversations between club members, Oreki's detached narration playing over the gentle river below.

ingredients.name: Fudo Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: Fudo Bridge over the Miya River appears in episode 21 during the Kanya Festival arc, where its riverside setting is used for a contemplative scene as Oreki works through the festival's central puzzle.

ingredients.name: Fujimiya
ingredients.localName: 二四三屋 鍛冶橋店
ingredients.entityContext: This traditional Hida confectionery near Kajibashi appears in episode 1, one of the first Takayama street locations glimpsed as Oreki reluctantly heads toward Kamiyama High School and the Classics Club.

ingredients.name: Garyuzakura
ingredients.entityContext: This ancient weeping cherry tree in Ichinomiya appears in episode 22, where its dramatic spring bloom provides a visually resonant backdrop for the season's emotional resolution and Oreki's quiet reflection.

ingredients.name: Hanazatokosen Bridge
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒506-0031 Gifu, Takayama, 一般国道158号
ingredients.entityContext: The Hanazato railway overpass near Takayama Station appears in episode 21, its iron lattice framing a key transitional scene as characters move through the city during the escalating Kanya Festival storyline.

ingredients.name: Hida High School (Bus)
ingredients.entityContext: The Hida Koko-mae bus stop appears in episode 5 as the transit point Oreki uses on his daily commute, reflecting the real-world Gifu High School bus stop that was the template for Kamiyama High School's approach route.

ingredients.name: Yayoisoba Kado
ingredients.entityContext: This Takayama soba restaurant near Nanukamachi appears in episode 1 as part of the town's downtown foodscape, its traditional noodle-shop exterior visible as Oreki makes his way through familiar streets on his first day.

ingredients.name: Yayoi Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: Yayoi Bridge over the Miya River is featured in the opening credits sequence, its simple railing and river view embodying the unhurried pace of Kamiyama that Oreki narrates with wry energy conservation.

ingredients.name: Yakiniku Kaede
ingredients.entityContext: This Honmachi yakiniku restaurant appears in episode 10 as one of the eateries Chitanda enlists Oreki to help investigate during a food-related mystery, the warm glow of its entrance lit against the evening street.

ingredients.name: Tronchi
ingredients.entityContext: This Gifu city clothing boutique appears in episode 21 as a background location during an out-of-town segment, its storefront used to establish the broader Gifu prefecture geography surrounding Kamiyama.

ingredients.name: Takayama Shiyakusho Kajibashi Parking Lot
ingredients.localName: 市営 かじ橋駐車場
ingredients.entityContext: The Kajibashi municipal parking lot near Honmachi appears in episode 11 as part of the downtown grid Oreki navigates while pursuing a line of inquiry, the utilitarian lot grounding the story in Takayama's everyday urban fabric.

ingredients.name: Takayama Kosei Hospital (Bus)
ingredients.entityContext: The Kosei Hospital bus stop in Takayama appears in episode 4, where its roadside location provides a realistic transit detail for a scene involving a character's journey across the city.

ingredients.name: Takayama City Swimming Pool
ingredients.entityContext: Takayama's public swimming pool appears in the OVA episode, where club members spend a summer afternoon at the pool and the scene delivers a rare slice-of-life reprieve from mystery-solving, with Oreki reluctantly drawn in.

ingredients.name: Takayama City Main Library
ingredients.localName: 高山市図書館 煥章館(本館)
ingredients.entityContext: The Kanshokan main library appears in episode 18 as the research destination Oreki and Chitanda visit to dig through local records, its reading rooms perfectly suiting the show's studious, archive-hunting tone.

ingredients.name: Takanoyudai 3 Parking Lot
ingredients.entityContext: This parking area near the Enako-gawa riverbank appears in episode 4 as a background transitional location while characters travel between Takayama's central districts during an investigation sequence.

ingredients.name: Suzuya Restaurant
ingredients.entityContext: Suzuya, a long-standing Takayama Japanese restaurant, appears in episode 15 as the dining spot where club members share a meal, its traditional Hida cuisine atmosphere reinforcing the show's deep roots in local Gifu culture.

ingredients.name: Suwa Shrine
ingredients.address: 宮山-1 Nakasu, Suwa, Nagano 392-0015, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Suwa Shrine grounds in episode 5 serve as the location where Oreki meets a key witness during an investigation, the shrine's forested approach adding a solemn weight to the conversation.

ingredients.name: Sushi Sho
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒534-0024 Osaka, Miyakojima Ward, Higashinodamachi, 3 Chome−10−19 京橋サンピアザビル 地下2階
ingredients.entityContext: This sushi establishment near Hanakawamachi appears in episode 11 as a background storefront in downtown Takayama, the traditional signage blending into the merchant-town streetscape Oreki moves through.

ingredients.name: Sugi Pharmacy Takayama Honmachi Store
ingredients.localName: スギ薬局 高山本町店
ingredients.entityContext: The Sugi Pharmacy on Honmachi appears in episode 4, its modern drugstore facade standing in mild contrast to the historic shopfronts around it and used as a navigational landmark during an errands scene.

ingredients.name: Shioya Liquor Store
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒606-8412 Kyoto, Sakyo Ward, Jodoji Banbacho, ６７−67
ingredients.entityContext: This neighborhood liquor shop near the Enako River appears in episode 4 as a background business Oreki passes, one of many small Takayama storefronts that Hyouka uses to paint a believable, lived-in version of Kamiyama.

ingredients.name: Shimojima Produce Store
ingredients.entityContext: The Shimojima greengrocer on Honmachi appears in episode 9, its fresh produce stall a visual anchor for the shopping-street segment where Chitanda pursues a curiosity about a vendor's unusual behavior.

ingredients.name: Sanso Yunosato
ingredients.address: 119-1 Okuhida Onsengo Hirayu, Takayama, Gifu 506-1433, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This traditional inn near Hirayu Onsen appears in episode 7 as one of the ryokan-area buildings that frame the class trip setting, its mountain lodge exterior evoking the remote Okuhida resort atmosphere.

ingredients.name: Sakuragaokahachiman Shrine
ingredients.entityContext: This Yamaguchimachi shrine appears in episode 4 during a scene where Oreki follows a lead to the eastern outskirts of town, the shrine's modest grounds providing a quiet counterpoint to the investigation's mounting tension.

ingredients.name: SORAMACHI: A private inn by the river
ingredients.localName: 川沿いの貸切一軒宿 SORAMACHI
ingredients.entityContext: The riverside building that inspired this guesthouse appears in episode 4 as a distinctive riverside structure in the Soyuji-machi area, its waterside position adding to the atmospheric geography of Kamiyama's canal district.

ingredients.name: Miyagawa Morning Markets
ingredients.entityContext: The famous Miyagawa Morning Market along the river is shown in the opening credits, its rows of vendor stalls and the riverbank bustle representing the living heartbeat of Takayama that Hyouka weaves into its Kamiyama backdrop.

ingredients.name: Miyagawa Morning Market Coffee Shop
ingredients.type: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店
categories: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店
ingredients.entityContext: This small riverside coffee stand at the morning market appears in the opening sequence, its casual outdoor seating along the Miya River embodying the leisurely small-city mood that Oreki reluctantly inhabits every morning.

ingredients.name: Minashi Shrine
ingredients.entityContext: Minashi Shrine, the Hida region's ichinomiya grand shrine, appears in episode 22 as a climactic pilgrimage destination, its sacred grove and stone torii lending gravitas to the season's final mystery resolution.

ingredients.name: Koujiya Shibata Haruji Shoten
ingredients.localName: 糀屋柴田春次商店 朝市店
ingredients.entityContext: This morning-market miso and koji shop is featured in the opening credits among the Miyagawa market stalls, its handcrafted fermented goods representing the artisan food culture that colors Kamiyama's daily life.

ingredients.name: Kawara Shunkai Studio
ingredients.entityContext: This Hida lacquerware workshop near Enakomachi appears in episode 4 as the craft studio a character visits while researching local traditional arts, Hyouka using Takayama's renowned lacquer heritage as a story detail.
